source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - High Island A368 Platform

event.top_event: Battery explosion with chemical burn injury
actions_taken: Lead Operator flushed IP's face and eyes with water
High Island A368 'B' facility contacted to request SAR Helicopter
IP evacuated by SAR Helicopter to Lake Charles Memorial Hospital
IP released from Doctors' care to full duty on 12-Aug-2014
event.summary: On 06-AUG-2014 at 0200 hours, the main platform generator at Energy XXI's High Island A368 'A' platform shut down due to severed belts. Two contract operators attempted to start the backup diesel generator. When the 'A' Operator removed the cover from the second battery box to check terminal connections, sparks from a loose battery terminal ignited accumulated hydrogen gas in the battery box, causing an explosion. Battery acid was blown into the operator's face and eyes. The operator was evacuated by helicopter and treated for chemical burns.

hazards.name: Hydrogen gas accumulation in battery box

threats.name: Sparks from loose battery terminal igniting hydrogen gas
Operator contact with battery acid during explosion
Backup generator unavailable due to battery failure

consequences.name: Chemical burn injury to face and eyes
Loss of backup power generation capability

controls.name: Backup diesel generator with battery start system
controls.barrier_role: Provide redundant power source when main generator fails
controls.lod_basis: Secondary power system to maintain platform operations

supporting_text: The backup diesel generator utilizes two 12 volt direct current (VDC) batteries in series to start the generator's diesel engine
The Lead Operator pushed the start switch on the backup diesel generator and the starter engaged briefly then stopped

controls.name: Battery terminal inspection procedure
controls.barrier_role: Identify and correct loose battery connections before operation
controls.lod_basis: Preventive maintenance to detect loose connections

supporting_text: The 'A' Operator (Injured Party / IP) removed the cover from the first battery box to check for loose battery post terminal connections and they were secure
The IP then removed the second battery box cover to verify the terminals were secure
When the IP moved the battery terminal connections in battery box #2, it sparked

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) - face and eye protection
controls.barrier_role: Protect operator face and eyes from battery acid exposure during battery work
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to prevent injury from chemical exposure

supporting_text: The IP failed to wear Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) to protect face and eyes prior to checking the battery terminal connections on the backup diesel generator

The Lessee failed to perform all operations in a safe and workmanlike manner and provide for the preservation and conservation of property and the environment. The investigation revealed that operators were not wearing proper PPE at the time of the incident

controls.name: Hydrogen gas ventilation in battery box
controls.barrier_role: Prevent accumulation of hydrogen gas in battery enclosure
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent explosive atmosphere formation

supporting_text: The sparks from the loose battery terminal connection in battery box #2 are believed to have been the ignition source that caused an accumulation of hydrogen gas in battery box #2 to explode

controls.name: Ignition source control during battery maintenance
controls.barrier_role: Prevent sparks or ignition sources when working with batteries
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to eliminate ignition sources in explosive atmosphere

supporting_text: When the IP moved the battery terminal connections in battery box #2, it sparked
Sparks from a loose battery terminal connection on the #2 battery for the backup diesel generator are suspected to have ignited a hydrogen gas accumulation in the #2 battery box

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
